User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user profile in the Mystic Forge AI application, storing user-specific settings and references to their generated fortunes. This entity complements an external authentication system like Firebase Auth.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firebaseUid.description: Unique identifier provided by Firebase Authentication for the user. (Relationship: Firebase Auth 1:1 User)

subscriptionStatus.description: The current subscription status of the user (e.g., 'free', 'premium').

credits.description: The number of remaining credits the user has for premium features.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user's profile information was last updated.

Fortune.title: Fortune
Fortune.description: Represents a single generated blessing or prophecy, including the user's input data, the AI-generated text, and associated symbolic information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Fortune entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who requested this fortune. (Relationship: User 1:N Fortune)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this fortune was generated.

nameInput.description: The user's name provided as input for this specific fortune.

birthMonthInput.description: The user's birth month provided as input for this specific fortune.

intentionInput.description: The user's current intention provided as input for this specific fortune.

moodInput.description: The user's emotional state or mood provided as input for this specific fortune.

interestsInput.description: A list of user's interests provided as input for this specific fortune.

favoriteColorInput.description: The user's favorite color provided as input for this specific fortune.

locationInput.description: The user's location provided as input for this specific fortune (optional).

generatedBlessingText.description: The complete text of the AI-generated mystical blessing or prophecy.

generatedSymbols.description: A JSON string storing the symbolic transformations and omens derived from user input and other random factors (e.g., season, element, animalGuide, sacredSymbol, emotionalOmen, colorAura, oraclePersona, sacredNumber, guidingSymbol, animalOmen).

oraclePersonaUsed.description: The specific oracle persona (e.g., 'Forest Oracle', 'Star Oracle') used to generate this blessing.

sacredNumber.description: A sacred number generated and associated with this fortune.

guidingSymbol.description: A general guiding symbol generated for this fortune (e.g., 'moon', 'river', 'gate').

animalOmen.description: An animal omen generated for this fortune (e.g., 'crane', 'fox', 'owl').

isPremiumFortune.description: Indicates whether this fortune was generated using premium features or credits.

shareImageUrl.description: A URI to a shareable image generated for this fortune, suitable for social media.

definition.description: Stores user profile data, private to the authenticated user. The document ID (`userId`) must match `request.auth.uid`. Includes 'firebaseUid' which serves as a denormalized identifier for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores individual blessings/fortunes generated for a specific user. This data is private to the user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring the document's owner can be verified directly without parent document lookups.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this fortune, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: fortuneId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific generated fortune.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for Mystic Forge AI is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence and Query Accessibility Patterns (QAPs), ensuring robust security rules and efficient data access.

1.  **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored in a top-level collection `/users`. Each document's ID (`userId`) is mandated to be the Firebase Authentication UID of the respective user. This structure inherently ensures that access to a user's profile is restricted to that user (`request.auth.uid == userId`). Authorization independence is achieved because the document's ID directly serves as the authorization key, eliminating the need for `get()` operations to verify ownership. This also supports QAPs as any `list` or `get` operation must specify the `userId` in the path, which will be matched against `request.auth.uid`, thus ensuring that only authorized data is retrieved.

2.  **User Fortunes (`/users/{userId}/fortunes/{fortuneId}`):** Fortunes are stored as a subcollection under their respective user's document. This maintains a clear hierarchical ownership model (`User 1:N Fortune`). To achieve **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL)**, every `Fortune` document includes a denormalized `userId` field. This `userId` field explicitly stores the Firebase UID of the fortune's owner, mirroring the parent document's ID and the authenticated user's UID. This denormalization allows security rules to evaluate `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` directly on the `Fortune` document itself, without needing to perform a `get()` call to the parent `/users/{userId}` document. This enables atomic writes (e.g., creating a fortune without first needing to verify the parent user) and drastically simplifies security rules, making them more performant and easier to debug. For **QAPs**, `list` queries for fortunes are intrinsically secure; a user can only query fortunes within their own `/users/{userId}/fortunes` path, where `{userId}` must match `request.auth.uid`. This design prevents accidental data exposure and ensures that `list` operations never return unauthorized data, adhering to the principle that 'rules are not filters'.

This design prioritizes clarity, security, and performance by leveraging path-based ownership, structural segregation, and strategic denormalization of authorization attributes, minimizing rule complexity and supporting scalable development.
